sources.name: DFL Bundesliga Data Shootout (Kaggle competition data)
sources.content: Originally: broadcast-style recordings of 9 Bundesliga games split into halves plus ~30s event clips (the 0bfacc_0.mp4-style IDs). NO LONGER HOSTED.
sources.license_terms: Verified from the live Rules page (Section 7): 'You may access and use the Competition Data only for participating in the Competition and on Kaggle.com forums.' And explicitly: 'To avoid any further doubt, you are not eligible to use the Competition Data for further developments, publications or any purposes after the end of the Competition. You have to delete the Competition Data and all copies made thereof after the end of the Competition.' Section 7.B adds: 'You agree not to transmit, duplicate, publish, redistribute or otherwise provide or make available the Competition Data to any party not participating in the Competition.' This competition did NOT use Kaggle's common 'non-commercial/academic research' variant — DFL imposed competition-use-only plus a deletion obligation. Additionally, the Data tab now states verbatim: 'NOTE: DATA IS NO LONGER AVAILABLE FOR THIS COMPLETED COMPETITION.' Separately verified: DFL's only other open release, the IDSSE dataset (Nature Scientific Data 2025, 7 matches of 22/23 Bundesliga/2.Bundesliga), contains positional+event data only — no video.
sources.usable_for_spike: no — the files have been removed from Kaggle, and even archived copies are use-restricted to the competition with a mandatory post-competition deletion clause. Beware the third-party Kaggle re-upload 'saberghaderi/-dfl-bundesliga-460-mp4-videos-in-30sec-csv' — it is competition data redistributed in violation of Section 7.B; do not use it.
sources.download_method: kaggle competitions download -c dfl-bundesliga-data-shootout — this will NOT return the video files anymore (data tab says data is no longer available). No legitimate download path exists.

sources.name: roboflow/sports demo videos (examples/soccer/setup.sh)
sources.access: github.com/roboflow/sports — examples/soccer/setup.sh pulls 5 mp4s from Roboflow's Google Drive
sources.content: Five ~30s 1080p broadcast clips: 0bfacc_0.mp4, 2e57b9_0.mp4, 08fd33_0.mp4, 573e61_0.mp4, 121364_0.mp4. The hex-ID filenames match the DFL Kaggle competition's clip naming, and the repo README states training data 'originates from the DFL - Bundesliga Data Shootout Kaggle competition' — i.e., these ARE DFL competition clips re-hosted on Roboflow's Drive.
sources.license_terms: The repo's LICENSE is MIT (verified via GitHub API: 'Permission is hereby granted, free of charge... to deal in the Software without restriction'). But MIT covers the CODE only; there is no license grant anywhere in the repo for the video files, and the underlying footage is DFL competition data subject to the competition-use-only + deletion + no-redistribution rules quoted above. Roboflow re-hosting it does not confer rights.
sources.usable_for_spike: no — high risk. Despite being the de-facto community demo set, the footage rights were never granted for use outside the Kaggle competition, and a commercial company benchmarking against Bundesliga rights-holder footage is exactly the bad fact pattern. Use clips you have actual rights to instead.
sources.download_method: For reference only (do not use for the spike): gdown -O data/0bfacc_0.mp4 'https://drive.google.com/uc?id=12TqauVZ9tLAv8kWxTTBFWtgt2hNQ4_ZF' (and IDs 19PGw55V8aA6GZu5-Aac5_9mCy3fNxmEf, 1OG8K6wqUw9t7lp9ms1M48DxRhwTYciK-, 1yYPKuXbHsCxqjA9G-S6aeR2Kcnos8RPU, 1vVwjW1dE1drIdd4ZSILfbCGPD4weoNiu for the other four) — full script at https://raw.githubusercontent.com/roboflow/sports/main/examples/soccer/setup.sh

sources.name: SoccerNet (videos)
sources.access: soccer-net.org/data — broadcast videos gated behind a Google Form + KAUST NDA; annotations/labels are public via the SoccerNet pip package
sources.content: Broadcast footage of 500+ full professional matches (224p/720p), plus tracking/ReID/calibration subsets. Largest academic soccer video corpus.
sources.license_terms: Verified: the site states 'To download any video data, please first fill in this NDA (Non-Disclosure Agreement) form.' The form's binding checkboxes (verified on the live Google Form) include: 'research and non commercial use only', 'dataset and the videos are protected by copyrights', and 'not to share the dataset with anyone else'. The linked NDA PDF (downloaded and read) is a KAUST Non-Disclosure Agreement whose Section 3.b restricts use 'solely for purposes authorized in Exhibit A (the Purposes)'. Non-video annotations do not require the NDA.
sources.usable_for_spike: no for the video data — an internal evaluation at a commercial company to benchmark a commercial product is not 'research and non commercial use', and signing the NDA on behalf of the company would be a misrepresentation. Conditional path: email the SoccerNet/KAUST team for written permission for commercial evaluation; some labs grant it. The public annotations (no video) are fine to use.
sources.download_method: Only after NDA: pip install SoccerNet; then in Python: from SoccerNet.Downloader import SoccerNetDownloader; d = SoccerNetDownloader(LocalDirectory='./SoccerNet'); d.password='<from NDA email>'; d.downloadGames(files=['1_720p.mkv'], split=['test'])

sources.name: SoccerTrack v2 (best source — verified CC BY 4.0 incl. commercial)
sources.access: https://atomscott.github.io/SoccerTrack-v2/ + github.com/AtomScott/SoccerTrack-v2; data mirror: Google Drive folder 1N2Qx2qkFgRtpbHitl2Vh6sLVYGgqkWwn (verified live, HTTP 200). The listed Hugging Face repo (atomscott/soccertrack-v2) currently returns 401/not public — use the Drive mirror or repo scripts.
sources.content: 10 full-length Japanese university-level amateur matches (~900 minutes total), 4K panoramic full-pitch video (BePro Cerberus for 2 matches, 3-camera stitched panorama for 8), MP4 per half, with per-frame GSR/MOT/ball-action annotations. This is literally college/amateur footage from Japan — a direct match for the product's target.
sources.license_terms: Verified from the repo's LICENSE-DATA file (CC BY 4.0 full text): you may use the material 'for any purpose, even commercially', provided you 'give appropriate credit, provide a link to the licence, and indicate if changes were made'. Project page states: 'The dataset (videos and annotations) is released under CC BY 4.0. Both permit academic and commercial use, with attribution.' Code is MIT. (The older SoccerTrack v1 fisheye/drone dataset on Kaggle has an unverified license behind a sign-in wall — skip it, v2 supersedes it.)
sources.usable_for_spike: yes — CC BY 4.0 explicitly permits commercial use; internal evaluation is comfortably covered. Keep an attribution note (cite arXiv:2508.01802) in the eval repo.
sources.download_method: git clone https://github.com/AtomScott/SoccerTrack-v2.git && cd SoccerTrack-v2 && uv sync (repo provides download/ground-truth scripts, e.g. ./scripts/create_ground_truth.sh <match_id>); or grab videos directly from the Drive mirror: gdown --folder 'https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1N2Qx2qkFgRtpbHitl2Vh6sLVYGgqkWwn'

sources.name: CC-BY YouTube amateur/college footage (5 videos, each individually verified)
sources.access: YouTube; every video below was checked on its live watch page for the exact string 'Creative Commons Attribution license (reuse allowed)' (= CC BY 3.0) on 2026-06-10
sources.content: (1) Ryt6tidyYaI 'AFC Yorkies 4-0 AFC Wyke | Full Match unedited | VEO 2 Camera | University of Hull' — 1:55:22, 1080p, VEO auto-panning sideline camera, UK university/grassroots. (2) bcoAMvp9ez8 'Bayhawks Men's Soccer vs Massasoit' (Bristol Community College channel) — 1:47:08, 1080p, US college soccer. (3) yVMpFuMD71I 'Bayhawk Women's soccer vs Monroe College' — 1:34:34, 1080p, US college women's. (4) CqL3iTdRUlg 'Full Match Volsungur Vs Sindri VEO' — 1:52:47, 1080p, Icelandic lower-division, VEO panning cam. (5) C09BiuotZVM 'Bo'ness United v Linlithgow Rose Full Match' (club's official channel) — 1:11:16, 720p, Scottish semi-pro. No CC-flagged long-form Japanese university or JFL footage was found (searched 大学サッカー/JFL/フルマッチ with the CC filter — those channels don't apply CC).
sources.license_terms: YouTube's CC BY option means 'Creative Commons Attribution license (reuse allowed)' — CC BY 3.0, which permits commercial reuse with attribution. Verified per-video via the license field embedded in each watch page, not assumed from search filters. All five are the clubs'/colleges' own channels recording their own matches (AFC Yorkies, Bristol Community College, FootballInItaly/Volsungur, Bo'ness United FC Official), so the uploader-owns-rights assumption is reasonable; standard CC caveat that the licensor must actually hold the rights.
sources.usable_for_spike: yes — CC BY 3.0 covers internal commercial evaluation easily (it even covers redistribution with attribution, which you don't need). Record video ID + channel + license string at download time as your attribution/provenance log.
sources.download_method: brew install yt-dlp (not currently installed locally), then per video, with a guard that aborts if the license metadata is not CC: yt-dlp -f 'bv*[height<=1080]+ba/b' --match-filter "license~='(?i)creative commons'" -o '%(id)s_%(title).60s.%(ext)s' --write-info-json 'https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Ryt6tidyYaI' (repeat for bcoAMvp9ez8, yVMpFuMD71I, CqL3iTdRUlg, C09BiuotZVM). --write-info-json preserves the license field as evidence. To find more: YouTube search with &sp=EgQYAjAB (CC + >20min filter).

sources.name: Alfheim / Tromsø IL dataset (Simula)
sources.access: https://datasets.simula.no/alfheim/ (direct HTTP downloads, ~100 GB; note the site currently serves a broken TLS chain — curl -k or fix CA path)
sources.content: 2 full Tippeligaen games + 1 ~40min friendly (Tromsø IL vs Strømsgodset, Anzhi, Tottenham, 2013) — three individual wide-angle cameras plus stitched cylindrical panorama, delivered as 3-second H.264 segments, with synced ZXY 20Hz player-position sensor data and some ball annotations.
sources.license_terms: Verified from the live page: 'The data may only be used for non-commercial research purposes.' Also: 'attempts to re-identify individual players, create player/club performance profiles, and similar, are not allowed.'
sources.usable_for_spike: no — 'non-commercial research purposes' excludes benchmarking a commercial product inside a company. Conditional path: the page invites contact (paalh at simula dot no) and 'always welcome[s] collaboration' — written permission would flip this, but as published the terms don't allow it. SoccerTrack v2 gives you equivalent panorama footage under CC BY anyway.
sources.download_method: (if permission obtained) wget -r -np https://datasets.simula.no/downloads/alfheim/2013-11-03/First%20Half/panorama/ etc.; segments concatenate with ffmpeg -f concat

sources.name: ISSIA-CNR Soccer dataset
sources.access: Original host www.issia.cnr.it/wp/dataset-cnr-fig/ — dead (confirmed dead since at least 2019 per GitHub issues on HaziqRazali/Soccer-Tracker and jac99/FootAndBall); only unofficial mirrors circulate
sources.content: Six static 25fps full-HD cameras, ~3,000 annotated frames (~2 min) of one professional match — tiny, old, and only useful for detector sanity checks anyway
sources.license_terms: UNVERIFIABLE — the official site and any license text are gone; mirrors carry no terms. No verified license exists to rely on.
sources.usable_for_spike: no — cannot establish any usage rights, and the content is too short to be worth the ambiguity.
sources.download_method: none legitimate (only unofficial re-uploads of unknown provenance)

spike_plan: Use two footage sets, both fully cleared. (1) Throughput + accuracy benchmarking: SoccerTrack v2 (CC BY 4.0, commercial OK) — 10 full university-level matches, ~900 min of 4K panoramic full-pitch video WITH per-frame tracking annotations, so it covers both the throughput stress test (4K, full halves) and accuracy measurement against ground truth without needing any NDA. Pull 2-3 matches from the Google Drive mirror to start. (2) Amateur-degradation testing: the verified CC-BY YouTube ladder — VEO auto-panning sideline cam at 1080p (Ryt6tidyYaI, University of Hull grassroots; CqL3iTdRUlg, Icelandic lower division), static-ish US college cam at 1080p (bcoAMvp9ez8 men's, yVMpFuMD71I women's — directly relevant to the college-soccer target), and 720p semi-pro (C09BiuotZVM) as the low-quality floor; degrade further synthetically with ffmpeg (downscale/bitrate-crush) rather than hunting for worse sources. The Japanese-football bonus is partially covered: SoccerTrack v2 IS Japanese university football; no CC-flagged JFL broadcast footage exists on YouTube. Do NOT use the DFL Kaggle data, the roboflow demo clips (they are re-hosted DFL competition clips), SoccerNet videos, Alfheim, or ISSIA for this spike.
licensing_summary: SAFE: SoccerTrack v2 (CC BY 4.0, LICENSE-DATA verified — 'any purpose, even commercially', attribution + cite arXiv:2508.01802) and the five individually-verified CC-BY YouTube videos ('Creative Commons Attribution license (reuse allowed)' confirmed on each watch page — commercial reuse with attribution; keep --write-info-json provenance logs). NOT SAFE: DFL Kaggle Bundesliga Data Shootout — data has been removed from Kaggle ('DATA IS NO LONGER AVAILABLE FOR THIS COMPLETED COMPETITION') and the rules restricted use to 'only for participating in the Competition', required deletion of all copies after the competition, and banned redistribution — this also poisons the roboflow/sports demo clips (MIT covers the code only; the mp4s are re-hosted DFL competition clips with no license grant) and the third-party Kaggle re-upload of 460 clips. SoccerNet videos require a KAUST NDA whose form binds you to 'research and non commercial use only' and 'not to share the dataset with anyone else' — a commercial-company product evaluation does not qualify without separate written permission. Alfheim is verified 'non-commercial research purposes' only (plus a re-identification ban) — same problem. ISSIA-CNR is dead with no recoverable license text. Net: run the spike entirely on SoccerTrack v2 + the CC-BY YouTube set; both permit internal commercial evaluation outright, with attribution records kept.
